questions.question: A healthcare startup is planning to build a generative AI application to summarize patient notes. The development team needs to experiment with various high-performing foundation models (FMs) from different providers (such as AI21 Labs, Anthropic, Cohere, and Amazon) to find the best fit for their use case. They require a fully managed service that allows them to access these models via a single API without the operational burden of managing servers or physical infrastructure.Which AWS service should the company use to meet these requirements?
questions.explain: CORRECT: Amazon Bedrock Amazon Bedrock is a fully managed service that makes high-performing foundation models (FMs) from leading AI startups and Amazon available via a single API. It is designed to be the easiest way to build and scale generative AI applications with FMs, removing the need to manage physical infrastructure. INCORRECT: Amazon SageMaker JumpStart While Amazon SageMaker JumpStart provides access to foundation models, it is a machine learning (ML) hub that allows you to deploy models to SageMaker endpoints. It generally requires more management of the underlying compute resources (instances) compared to a serverless API experience. The scenario specifically asks for a fully managed service accessed via a single API without managing infrastructure, for which Amazon Bedrock is the purpose-built solution. INCORRECT: Amazon Q Business Amazon Q Business is a generative AI-powered assistant designed for business users to answer questions, provide summaries, and generate content based on enterprise data. It is a SaaS application/assistant, not a platform for developers to access raw foundation models via API to build their own custom applications. INCORRECT: Amazon Kendra Amazon Kendra is an intelligent enterprise search service powered by machine learning. While it uses NLP, it is designed for searching documents and indexing content, not for providing direct API access to a variety of foundation models for text generation tasks.

questions.question: A data science team has fine-tuned an open-source Large Language Model (LLM) using a specialized internal dataset. They now need to deploy this custom model into a production environment. The team requires full control over the underlying infrastructure, including the specific GPU instance types used for inference and the ability to configure custom auto-scaling policies.Which AWS service provides the capabilities to host this model while meeting these infrastructure requirements?
questions.explain: CORRECT: Amazon SageMaker AI Amazon SageMaker AI allows developers to build, train, and deploy machine learning models. It provides the capability to deploy custom or fine-tuned models to persistent endpoints (SageMaker Real-time Inference). This gives the team full control over the compute resources (selecting specific EC2 instance types like ml.g5 or ml.p4) and allows for granular configuration of auto-scaling policies. INCORRECT: Amazon Bedrock Amazon Bedrock is a fully managed service that offers access to foundation models via an API. While it supports fine-tuning for certain models, it abstracts away the underlying infrastructure. Users cannot select specific GPU instance types or manage the hosting servers directly, which contradicts the requirement for Ђ

INCORRECT: Amazon Comprehend Amazon Comprehend is a Natural Language Processing (NLP) service that uses pre-trained models to uncover information in text (like sentiment analysis or entity recognition). It is a high-level API service and is not designed for hosting custom, fine-tuned Large Language Models (LLMs) with infrastructure control. INCORRECT: Amazon Textract Amazon Textract is a machine learning service that automatically extracts text, handwriting, and data from scanned documents. It is a specialized OCR (Optical Character Recognition) service and is not used for deploying or hosting generative AI models.

refs.label: Amazon SageMaker - Deploy models for inference

questions.question: A financial services firm is building an internal chatbot to assist employees with inquiries about company policies. These policies are stored as hundreds of PDF documents in an Amazon S3 bucket and are updated weekly. The firm wants to ensure the chatbot always answers using the most current information without incurring the high cost and operational overhead of frequently retraining or fine-tuning the foundation model (FM).Which architecture pattern should the developer implement to meet these requirements?
options: Continuously fine-tune the foundation model (FM) on the new policy documents every week.
Implement Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) to retrieve relevant policy excerpts before generating an answer.
Use Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) to align the model's behavior with the updated policies.
Embed the full text of all policy documents directly into the system prompt for every API call.
questions.explain: CORRECT: Implement Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) to retrieve relevant policy excerpts before generating an answer. RAG is the standard pattern for connecting foundation models to external, private, or frequently updating data sources. By indexing the documents (e.g., in a vector store), the application can retrieve only the relevant information needed for a specific query and pass it to the FM as context. This ensures the answers are up-to-date without retraining the model. Disclaimer: This is a high-level conceptual diagram illustrating the logical flow of RAG. Focus on the sequence (Retrieve -> Augment  -> Generate) . In production, steps like Ђ

may involve complex sub-architectures (e.g., Knowledge Bases or Lambda). ---- INCORRECT: Continuously fine-tune the foundation model (FM) on the new policy documents every week. Fine-tuning is computationally expensive, time-consuming, and generally not recommended for data that changes frequently (like weekly policy updates). It is better suited for adapting a model's style or teaching it a specific task or domain language rather than acting as a dynamic knowledge base. INCORRECT: Use Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) to align the model's behavior with the updated policies. RLHF is primarily used to align a model's behavior with human preferences (e.g., to be more helpful or harmless) or to reduce toxicity. It is not an efficient mechanism for injecting new factual knowledge or keeping up with frequently changing documents. INCORRECT: Embed the full text of all policy documents directly into the system prompt for every API call. While FMs have context windows, placing hundreds of documents into every prompt is likely to exceed the token limit (context window) of the model. Even if the window allows it, this approach would be extremely expensive and slow (high latency) due to the large number of input tokens required for every single question.

refs.label: Retrieve data and generate AI responses with Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases

questions.question: A lead developer is presenting a Generative AI strategy to company executives. The CTO asks for clarification regarding the terminology used in the proposal, specifically the difference between Ђ
(FMs) and Ђ
(LLMs).Which statement accurately describes the relationship between these two terms?
options: Foundation Models are exclusively used for generating images and audio, whereas LLMs are exclusively used for text processing.
is a legacy term used for older AI models, whereas Ђ
refers to the modern architecture used in services like Amazon Bedrock.
LLMs are a specific subset of Foundation Models that are trained on massive amounts of text data to understand and generate human language.
Foundation Models are small, task-specific models that require training from scratch, whereas LLMs are pre-trained and ready to use.
questions.explain: CORRECT: LLMs are a specific subset of Foundation Models that are trained on massive amounts of text data to understand and generate human language. A Foundation Model is a broad class of models trained on vast amounts of data that can be adapted to a wide range of tasks. Large Language Models (LLMs) are a type of Foundation Model specifically specialized in text-to-text tasks. For example, Amazon Titan Text is an LLM, which falls under the umbrella of Foundation Models available in Amazon Bedrock. INCORRECT:  Foundation Models are exclusively used for generating images and audio, whereas LLMs are exclusively used for text processing. Foundation Models is a broad category that encompasses models capable of handling various modalities, including text, images, audio, and video. Therefore, FMs are not limited to just images and audio; they includes text-based models as well. INCORRECT:  Ђ
is a legacy term used for older AI models, whereas Ђ
refers to the modern architecture used in services like Amazon Bedrock. Ђ

is the current, standard industry term (and the term used throughout AWS documentation and the AIP-C01 exam) for large-scale models trained on broad data that can be adapted to downstream tasks. It is not a legacy term. INCORRECT:  Foundation Models are small, task-specific models that require training from scratch, whereas LLMs are pre-trained and ready to use. This is the opposite of the truth. Foundation Models are named Ђ
because they serve as the base (or foundation) upon which many different applications can be built without training from scratch. They are typically very large and pre-trained.

questions.question: A healthcare company is building a generative AI chatbot to summarize patient feedback collected via a web form. To comply with strict privacy regulations (HIPAA), the company must ensure that all Personally Identifiable Information (PII)Ѓ\such as names, phone numbers, and addressesЃ\is identified and redacted from the text before the data is sent to the foundation model (FM) for summarization.Which AWS service should the developer use to accurately detect these PII entities within the text data?
questions.explain: CORRECT: Amazon Comprehend Amazon Comprehend is a natural language processing (NLP) service that uses machine learning to uncover information in text. It specifically includes features to detect Personally Identifiable Information (PII) in text documents. This allows developers to identify the location of sensitive entities and redact them before passing the clean text to an LLM, ensuring privacy compliance. INCORRECT: Amazon Macie Amazon Macie is a data security service that uses machine learning to discover and protect sensitive data stored in Amazon S3 . While it detects PII, it is designed for scanning storage buckets at scale to identify risks, rather than processing individual text strings or documents in real-time pipelines for Generative AI applications. INCORRECT: Amazon Textract Amazon Textract is a machine learning service designed to extract text, handwriting, and data from scanned documents and images (OCR). While it extracts text, its primary purpose is digitization, not the semantic analysis or PII classification of raw text strings. INCORRECT: Amazon Inspector Amazon Inspector is an automated vulnerability management service that continually scans AWS workloads (like EC2 instances, Lambda functions, and container images) for software vulnerabilities and unintended network exposure. It does not analyze text data for PII.

questions.question: A legal firm wants to build a Generative AI application that allows lawyers to ask natural language questions about historical case files. These case files currently exist as millions of scanned PDF images stored in an Amazon S3 bucket. Before the foundation model (FM) can process and index this information for a RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) workflow, the text needs to be extracted from these scanned documents.Which AWS service is purpose-built to automatically extract text, handwriting, and data from these scanned documents?
questions.explain: CORRECT: Amazon Textract Amazon Textract is a machine learning service that automatically extracts text, handwriting, and data from scanned documents. In a Generative AI pipeline, Textract is the essential first step for unlocking data trapped in image-based files (like scanned PDFs) so that the text can be chunked, embedded, and fed into a Foundation Model. INCORRECT: Amazon Transcribe Amazon Transcribe is an automatic speech recognition (ASR) service that converts audio to text. It is used for processing voice recordings, meeting notes, or customer service calls, not for extracting text from visual documents like PDFs or images. INCORRECT: Amazon Comprehend Amazon Comprehend is a natural language processing (NLP) service that uncovers insights and relationships in text. However, it requires the input to be text already. It cannot Ђ

a scanned image; it needs the output from a service like Amazon Textract to perform its analysis. INCORRECT: Amazon Polly Amazon Polly is a service that turns text into lifelike speech. It is used for creating audio from text applications, which is the reverse of what is needed in this scenario.

refs.label: Amazon Textract is a machine learning (ML) service that uses OCR to automatically extract text, handwriting, and data from scanned documents such as PDFs.

questions.question: A retail company is enhancing its mobile app with a Ђ

feature. The backend workflow uses an Amazon Bedrock embedding model to convert product descriptions into vector embeddings. The development team needs a fully managed, scalable service to store these embeddings and perform low-latency k-nearest neighbor (k-NN) searches to retrieve similar items in real-time.Which AWS service is purpose-built to act as the vector database for this solution?
questions.explain: CORRECT: Amazon OpenSearch Service Amazon OpenSearch Service (specifically with its k-NN plugin or the Serverless Vector Engine) is a fully managed service that supports vector storage and similarity search. It allows you to index embeddings and perform efficient k-nearest neighbor (k-NN) searches to find semantically similar items, making it the standard choice for this requirement. INCORRECT: Amazon Athena Amazon Athena is an interactive query service that makes it easy to analyze data in Amazon S3 using standard SQL. While it can query data, it is designed for analytical scanning of large datasets (OLAP), not for low-latency, real-time vector similarity searches required for a user-facing application. INCORRECT: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams Amazon Kinesis Data Streams is a serverless streaming data service used to capture, process, and store data streams in real-time. It is a transport mechanism for moving data (ingestion), not a database for indexing vectors or performing similarity queries. INCORRECT: Amazon S3 Intelligent-Tiering Amazon S3 is an object storage service. While it can store the files containing embeddings, it provides no native capability to index them or perform vector math (like cosine similarity) to find Ђ
You cannot run a semantic search query directly against S3 storage classes.

refs.label: Amazon OpenSearch ServiceЃfs vector database capabilities explained

questions.question: A developer is building a custom question-answering application using Amazon Bedrock. The application must answer user queries based on a specific set of technical manuals stored in Amazon S3. The developer wants to implement a Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) workflow but wants to minimize the operational overhead of manually setting up data ingestion pipelines, chunking text, generating embeddings, and managing a vector database.Which Amazon Bedrock feature should the developer use to achieve this?
questions.explain: CORRECT: Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases is a fully managed capability that helps you implement RAG. It automates the end-to-end workflow of ingesting data from a source (like S3), chunking it, converting it into embeddings, storing it in a vector database, and retrieving relevant data to augment the Foundation Model's response. This removes the need for manual pipeline coding. INCORRECT: Amazon Bedrock Guardrails Amazon Bedrock Guardrails is a feature designed to implement safety controls, such as filtering harmful content or blocking specific topics and PII. It is not used for ingesting data or managing retrieval workflows for RAG. INCORRECT: Amazon Bedrock Prompt Management Amazon Bedrock Prompt Management is a tool for creating, testing, and versioning prompts. While essential for tuning how the model behaves, it does not handle document ingestion, embedding generation, or vector storage. INCORRECT: Amazon Bedrock Agents Amazon Bedrock Agents are used to execute multi-step tasks and interact with external systems (APIs) to perform actions. While an Agent can query a Knowledge Base, the specific feature described in the scenario that handles the data ingestion, embedding, and vector management is the Ђ
itself.

refs.label: Retrieve data and generate AI responses with Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases
Knowledge Bases now delivers fully managed RAG experience in Amazon Bedrock

questions.question: A financial technology company is developing a customer support chatbot using Amazon Bedrock. The development team needs to ensure that the chatbot strictly adheres to company policies by blocking harmful content, redacting personally identifiable information (PII), and preventing the model from providing investment advice. They require a solution that applies these safety controls consistently across multiple foundation models (FMs) within their application. Which Amazon Bedrock feature should the developers implement to meet these requirements?
questions.explain: CORRECT: Amazon Bedrock Guardrails Amazon Bedrock Guardrails is the correct feature for implementing safeguards. It allows developers to define a set of safety policiesЃ\such as denied topics (e.g., investment advice), content filters (e.g., hate speech), and PII redactionЃ	hat are applied to both user inputs and model outputs. Crucially, these guardrails can be applied across different foundation models, ensuring consistent safety and governance. INCORRECT: Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases Knowledge Bases are used to implement Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG). They connect foundation models to internal data sources (like manuals or FAQs) to improve the accuracy and relevance of responses, but they are not primarily designed to enforce safety policies or block specific topics/content. INCORRECT: Amazon Bedrock Agents Agents are designed to execute multi-step tasks and orchestrate workflows by interacting with company systems and data sources. While they can follow instructions, they are not the specific feature dedicated to defining granular safety policies and content filtering definitions like Guardrails are. INCORRECT: Amazon Bedrock Prompt Management Prompt Management is used to create, save, version, and share prompts. While good prompt engineering can influence model behavior, it does not provide the robust, independent safety layer for filtering content and redacting PII that Guardrails provides.

refs.label: Detect and filter harmful content by using Amazon Bedrock Guardrails

Location in the AWS console:  Amazon Bedrock -> Agents INCORRECT: Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases is a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) feature. It is used to connect foundation models to data sources for retrieving information to answer questions. While it can Ђ

information, it is not designed to execute actions or write updates to external APIs (like updating a package status). questions.question: A startup is building a Generative AI application that uses Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG). The team needs a vector store that supports highly structured relational data and SQL-based queries while also providing the ability to store embeddings and perform semantic search using the pgvector extension. Which AWS database service is best suited for this requirement?
questions.explain: CORRECT: Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL-Compatible Edition Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL-Compatible Edition is a relational database that supports the pgvector extension . This allows developers to store vector embeddings alongside structured relational data and perform vector similarity searches using standard SQL queries, making it ideal for this scenario. INCORRECT: Amazon DynamoDB Amazon DynamoDB is a NoSQL key-value database. While it can store embeddings as attributes, it does not natively support SQL queries or relational data models . It is typically used for low-latency unstructured data or conversation history, not for SQL-based vector search workflows requiring pgvector. INCORRECT: Amazon S3 Amazon S3 is an object storage service. While it can store vast amounts of documents and data , it is not a database. It does not provide an engine to run SQL queries or perform native vector similarity searches on its own without integrating with other services like Amazon Athena or OpenSearch. INCORRECT: Amazon Neptune Amazon Neptune is a graph database used to build knowledge graphs and navigate highly connected datasets . While it is powerful for finding relationships between entities, it is not primarily a relational database (SQL) and is not the standard choice when the requirement specifically calls for pgvector on relational data.

questions.question: A media company uses Amazon Bedrock to generate summaries of news articles. However, before a summary is published, it must be reviewed by a human editor to ensure accuracy and tone. If the editor approves it, the summary is published to the website; if rejected, it is archived for analysis. Which AWS service is best suited to orchestrate this workflow, including the automated generation and the manual approval step?
questions.explain: CORRECT: AWS Step Functions AWS Step Functions is a serverless orchestration service. It is specifically designed to manage state and workflows, including Ђ

patterns . It can trigger the AI model, pause the workflow to wait for a human review callback, and then branch logic (publish vs. archive) based on the reviewer's decision. INCORRECT: AWS Lambda AWS Lambda is a compute service used to run code . While it can execute the code to call the model, managing a long-running process that waits for human interaction (which could take hours or days) is difficult and cost-inefficient with Lambda due to its 15-minute timeout limit. INCORRECT: Amazon EventBridge Amazon EventBridge is a serverless event bus used to route events between services . While it can trigger actions when an event happens, it is not designed to manage stateful workflows, handle branching logic based on human decisions, or maintain the context of a transaction over long wait periods. INCORRECT: AWS Glue AWS Glue is a serverless data integration service used primarily for ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) jobs and data preparation . It is not designed for orchestrating application logic or human approval workflows.

refs.label: Deploying a workflow that waits for human approval in Step Functions

questions.question: A developer is building a Generative AI chatbot using Amazon Bedrock. Before sending a user's prompt to the foundation model, the application needs to run a short Python script to sanitize the text by removing special characters and formatting it into a specific JSON structure. This pre-processing step must run on-demand without provisioning or managing servers.Which AWS service is the most suitable for this task?
questions.explain: CORRECT: AWS Lambda AWS Lambda is a serverless compute service that lets you run code without provisioning or managing servers. It is the ideal choice for this scenario because it can execute the Python sanitization script in response to an event (the user input), run for a few milliseconds, and scale automatically. INCORRECT: Amazon EC2 Amazon EC2 provides virtual servers that require provisioning, configuration, and ongoing management (OS patching, scaling). Using a full EC2 instance for running a simple, short-lived sanitization script is an inefficient use of resources and adds unnecessary operational overhead compared to a serverless solution. INCORRECT: AWS Glue AWS Glue is a serverless data integration service used primarily for ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) workflows to prepare data for analytics. It is designed for processing large datasets in batch jobs, not for low-latency, real-time text processing for a chatbot interaction. INCORRECT: AWS Batch AWS Batch is designed for running batch computing workloads (like analyzing financial models or rendering images). It is optimized for long-running, computationally intensive jobs, not for the sub-second, real-time request/response processing required for a chatbot's pre-processing step.

refs.label: Write a custom parser Lambda function in Amazon Bedrock Agents

questions.question: A startup has deployed a Generative AI text summarization model on Amazon SageMaker. They want to make this model accessible to their mobile application users via a secure HTTPS endpoint. To prevent abuse and manage costs, the team needs to enforce strict rate limits (throttling) on the number of requests each user can send per minute.Which AWS service should they place in front of the model to handle these request limits and authentication?
questions.explain: CORRECT: Amazon API Gateway Amazon API Gateway is a fully managed service that makes it easy to create, publish, maintain, monitor, and secure APIs. In the context of GenAI, it is the standard Ђ

for applications to access backend models. Crucially, it provides native features for throttling and rate limiting , allowing the developer to restrict how many requests a user can make to control costs and prevent system overload. [Disclaimer]: This diagram is a high-level conceptual visualization designed specifically for Exam Preparation (AIP-C01) . It simplifies complex architectures to focus on the key logical flows and exam objectives you need to know. While it accurately reflects AWS best practices and patterns, a real-world production environment would require additional low-level configurations (e.g., specific IAM roles, VPC subnets, or WAF rules) not depicted here. INCORRECT: AWS Lambda AWS Lambda is a compute service used to run the application logic (e.g., pre-processing text or invoking the SageMaker endpoint). While Lambda runs code, it does not natively provide the infrastructure for managing API endpoints, throttling user traffic at the network edge, or managing API keys/usage plans. INCORRECT: Amazon SQS Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) is a message queuing service used to decouple microservices and handle asynchronous processing. While it helps with buffering requests, it does not provide an HTTP API endpoint for mobile users or enforce per-user rate limiting policies like API Gateway does. INCORRECT: AWS Direct Connect AWS Direct Connect is a networking service used to establish a dedicated private network connection from an on-premises data center to AWS. It is used for hybrid connectivity, not for managing public API endpoints or throttling HTTP requests for a mobile application.

or Ђ

across models. INCORRECT: D) AWS X-Ray AWS X-Ray is an observability service used to trace requests as they travel through your application's microservices. It is useful for debugging latency and identifying bottlenecks (e.g., Ђ

questions.question: A government agency is digitizing historical handwritten census records. The workflow uses Amazon Textract to extract text from scanned images, which is then fed into an Amazon Bedrock model to generate demographic summaries. To ensure historical accuracy, the agency requires that any data field extracted with a confidence score below 85% be routed to a human reviewer for verification before it is processed by the Foundation Model.Which solution should the developer implement to automate this conditional review process?
options: Use Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth to create a daily labeling job for all scanned images, regardless of the extraction quality.
Implement an Amazon Augmented AI (Amazon A2I) human review workflow. Configure the workflow to trigger a human loop specifically when the confidence score meets the activation condition.
Configure Amazon CloudWatch Alarms to monitor the ConfidenceScore metric. Use an Amazon SNS topic to email the jpeg file to a data entry clerk whenever the alarm breaches the threshold.
Use Amazon Bedrock Agents to invoke a Ђ
action group. Prompt the model to autonomously decide if the handwriting is illegible and call the action group to pause the system.
questions.explain: CORRECT: Implement an Amazon Augmented AI (Amazon A2I) human review workflow. Configure the workflow to trigger a human loop specifically when the confidence score meets the activation condition. Amazon A2I is explicitly designed for this Ђ

(HITL) use case during the inference phase. It allows developers to define a workflow that automatically routes predictions (from services like Textract or Rekognition) to human reviewers only when specific conditions (like low confidence) are met. It handles the user interface (Worker Task Template) and the consolidation of results. Disclaimer: This diagram is a simplified conceptual model intended for exam preparation. Some implementation details are intentionally omitted or abstracted to highlight the core logic relevant to AWS certification exams. INCORRECT: Use Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth to create a daily labeling job for all scanned images... SageMaker Ground Truth is primarily used to build training datasets for machine learning models (labeling data from scratch). While it involves humans, it is not designed for the real-time or near-real-time Ђ

loop required here. Reviewing all images would also be inefficient compared to reviewing only low-confidence ones. INCORRECT: Configure Amazon CloudWatch Alarms to monitor the ConfidenceScore metric... While this provides a notification, it does not provide a software framework or User Interface (UI) for the clerk to view the image, correct the specific text field, and merge the data back into the pipeline. A2I provides the necessary UI and workflow orchestration. INCORRECT: Use Amazon Bedrock Agents to invoke a Ђ

action group... While Bedrock Agents can facilitate human interaction, the trigger here is a specific numerical confidence score from an OCR service (Textract), not a conversational ambiguity. A2I is the purpose-built integration for document processing reviews based on confidence thresholds.

questions.question: A software development team is building a GenAI application on AWS. A junior developer needs assistance understanding how to properly format API requests to Amazon Bedrock. The developer wants an AI assistant that can explain AWS SDK code examples and suggest corrections directly within their IDE.Which AWS service should the developer use?
questions.explain: CORRECT : Amazon Q Developer Amazon Q Developer is an AI-powered coding assistant designed specifically for developers. It integrates directly into IDEs (like VS Code, IntelliJ, and PyCharm) to provide code suggestions, explain code, generate boilerplate code, and help with AWS SDK usage. This makes it the ideal tool for the junior developer who needs help understanding and formatting Amazon Bedrock API requests within their development environment. INCORRECT : Amazon Q Business Amazon Q Business is a generative AI-powered assistant designed for business users to answer questions, provide summaries, and generate content based on enterprise data sources (like SharePoint, Salesforce, or internal wikis). It is focused on business intelligence and knowledge retrieval, not on providing coding assistance or IDE integration for developers. INCORRECT : Amazon Bedrock Agents Amazon Bedrock Agents are designed to execute multi-step tasks by orchestrating workflows and interacting with external APIs and data sources. They are used for building agentic AI applications, not for providing coding assistance to developers in an IDE. INCORRECT : Amazon CodeGuru Amazon CodeGuru is a developer tool that uses machine learning to provide automated code reviews and application performance recommendations. While it can identify code quality issues and suggest optimizations, it does not provide the interactive, conversational coding assistance or real-time code generation capabilities that Amazon Q Developer offers within an IDE. Key Concepts: Amazon Q Developer = IDE integration, code generation, AWS SDK assistance Amazon Q Business = enterprise knowledge retrieval, business user focus Bedrock Agents = multi-step task orchestration, not developer tooling

questions.question: A developer is building a customer service chatbot that uses Amazon Bedrock to generate detailed responses to complex customer inquiries. Some queries require the foundation model to process large amounts of context and can take 10-15 minutes to generate comprehensive answers. The developer wants to use AWS Lambda to invoke the Bedrock API.What is the primary limitation the developer will face with this architecture?
options: AWS Lambda has a maximum execution timeout of 15 minutes, which may not be sufficient for very long-running FM inference tasks
AWS Lambda cannot invoke Amazon Bedrock APIs due to IAM permission restrictions
AWS Lambda does not support asynchronous invocation patterns required for foundation models
AWS Lambda charges per request regardless of execution time, making it cost-prohibitive for FM workloads
questions.explain: CORRECT : AWS Lambda has a maximum execution timeout of 15 minutes, which may not be sufficient for very long-running FM inference tasks AWS Lambda functions have a hard limit of 900 seconds (15 minutes) for execution time. If a foundation model inference takes longer than this, the Lambda function will timeout and fail. For workloads that consistently approach or exceed this limit, alternative compute options like AWS Step Functions with asynchronous processing, Amazon ECS, or EC2 should be considered. INCORRECT : AWS Lambda cannot invoke Amazon Bedrock APIs due to IAM permission restrictions Lambda functions can absolutely invoke Amazon Bedrock APIs when given the appropriate IAM permissions (like bedrock:InvokeModel). There are no inherent restrictions preventing Lambda from calling Bedrock services. INCORRECT : AWS Lambda does not support asynchronous invocation patterns required for foundation models Lambda fully supports both synchronous and asynchronous invocation patterns. You can invoke Lambda asynchronously and it can process long-running tasks in the background. However, the 15-minute timeout still applies regardless of invocation type. INCORRECT : AWS Lambda charges per request regardless of execution time, making it cost-prohibitive for FM workloads Lambda pricing is based on the number of requests AND the duration of execution (GB-seconds). Longer executions do cost more, but this is not the primary technical limitation preventing its use for very long-running FM tasks - the timeout limit is the blocker. Key Concepts: Lambda timeout limit = 900 seconds (15 minutes) maximum Long-running FM inference = consider Step Functions, ECS, or EC2 Lambda IAM permissions = bedrock:InvokeModel for Bedrock access

questions.question: An e-commerce company wants to expose their internal product recommendation system (powered by Amazon Bedrock) to their mobile application. They need a secure HTTPS endpoint that mobile clients can call to get personalized recommendations. The recommendation logic requires pre-processing user data before sending it to the foundation model.Which AWS architecture pattern should the developer implement?
options: Use Amazon API Gateway to create a REST API endpoint that triggers an AWS Lambda function to pre-process data and invoke Amazon Bedrock
Connect the mobile app directly to Amazon Bedrock using the AWS SDK with embedded IAM credentials
Deploy an Amazon EC2 instance with a public IP address to host a custom web server that calls Bedrock
Use Amazon S3 static website hosting with client-side JavaScript to invoke Bedrock APIs
questions.explain: CORRECT : Use Amazon API Gateway to create a REST API endpoint that triggers an AWS Lambda function to pre-process data and invoke Amazon Bedrock This is the standard serverless pattern for exposing foundation model capabilities to client applications. API Gateway provides the HTTPS endpoint, request validation, throttling, and authentication mechanisms. Lambda executes the pre-processing logic and invokes Bedrock. This architecture is secure, scalable, and follows AWS best practices for serverless GenAI applications. INCORRECT : Connect the mobile app directly to Amazon Bedrock using the AWS SDK with embedded IAM credentials Embedding IAM credentials (access keys) directly in a mobile application is a critical security risk. These credentials can be extracted from the app binary and misused. Client applications should never have direct AWS credentials; instead, they should call an API that uses temporary credentials or identity federation. INCORRECT : Deploy an Amazon EC2 instance with a public IP address to host a custom web server that calls Bedrock While this approach would technically work, it requires managing servers, patching, scaling, and increases operational overhead compared to the serverless API Gateway + Lambda pattern. It also doesn't provide the built-in features like throttling, caching, and request validation that API Gateway offers. INCORRECT : Use Amazon S3 static website hosting with client-side JavaScript to invoke Bedrock APIs S3 static hosting serves static content (HTML, CSS, JS) but cannot execute server-side logic or securely store credentials needed to call Bedrock. Client-side JavaScript would expose credentials and cannot perform the required pre-processing on the server side. Key Concepts: API Gateway + Lambda = standard pattern for exposing FM to clients Never embed IAM credentials in mobile/client applications Serverless architecture = no server management, auto-scaling, built-in security

questions.question: A legal document processing application needs to perform multiple sequential operations using Amazon Bedrock: first, extract key clauses from a contract, then summarize each clause, and finally generate a risk assessment based on the summaries. Each step depends on the output of the previous step, and the entire workflow can take 5-10 minutes to complete.Which AWS service is best suited to orchestrate this multi-step foundation model workflow?
questions.explain: CORRECT : AWS Step Functions Step Functions is a serverless orchestration service designed to coordinate multiple AWS services into workflows. It is ideal for this scenario because it can chain multiple Amazon Bedrock invocations sequentially, pass the output of one step as input to the next, handle errors and retries, and manage long-running workflows (up to 1 year). It provides built-in state management and visual workflow monitoring, making it the standard choice for orchestrating multi-step GenAI pipelines. INCORRECT : AWS Lambda While Lambda can invoke Amazon Bedrock and execute the logic for each individual step, it has a 15-minute execution timeout limit. More importantly, Lambda is not designed to orchestrate workflows or manage state transitions between multiple steps. You would need to manually implement retry logic, error handling, and state passing, which Step Functions provides out of the box. INCORRECT : Amazon EventBridge EventBridge is an event bus service used to route events between different AWS services and applications. While it can trigger Lambda functions or Step Functions in response to events, it is not designed to maintain state or orchestrate sequential, dependent operations where each step requires the output from the previous step. INCORRECT : Amazon SQS SQS is a message queuing service used to decouple microservices and buffer requests. While it can hold messages between processing steps, it does not provide workflow orchestration, conditional logic, or built-in error handling. You would need to build significant custom logic to manage the sequential dependencies and state transitions required for this multi-step workflow. Key Concepts: Step Functions = orchestrate multi-step workflows with state management Sequential FM calls = pass output from one step as input to next Long-running workflows = Step Functions supports up to 1 year execution time

questions.question: A financial services company receives real-time transaction data from multiple payment processors. Whenever a suspicious transaction occurs (flagged by their fraud detection system), they want to automatically invoke an Amazon Bedrock foundation model to generate a detailed risk analysis report and store it in Amazon S3. The solution must be event-driven and require minimal infrastructure management.Which AWS service should the developer use to trigger the foundation model invocation when suspicious transactions are detected?
options: AWS Lambda with a cron schedule
questions.explain: CORRECT : Amazon EventBridge EventBridge is a serverless event bus that makes it easy to connect applications using events. In this scenario, the fraud detection system can publish events to EventBridge whenever a suspicious transaction is detected. EventBridge can then automatically trigger a Lambda function (or Step Functions workflow) to invoke Amazon Bedrock and process the transaction. This provides a loosely coupled, event-driven architecture that scales automatically and requires no infrastructure management. INCORRECT : AWS Lambda with a cron schedule A cron schedule triggers Lambda functions at fixed time intervals (e.g., every 5 minutes, hourly). This is useful for batch processing but not for real-time, event-driven workflows. Suspicious transactions need to be analyzed immediately when they occur, not on a schedule. Using a scheduled Lambda would introduce delays and miss the real-time requirement. INCORRECT : Amazon Kinesis Data Streams Kinesis Data Streams is designed for processing streaming data at scale (like clickstreams, application logs, or IoT telemetry). While it can handle real-time data ingestion, it requires continuous consumers to read and process records. For discrete events like Ђ

EventBridge provides a simpler, more appropriate solution without the overhead of managing stream consumers. INCORRECT : Amazon SNS SNS is a pub/sub messaging service that can send notifications to subscribers (email, SMS, HTTP endpoints). While SNS can trigger Lambda functions, EventBridge is better suited for application-to-application event routing because it supports advanced filtering, transformation rules, and integration with many AWS services. EventBridge also provides a centralized event bus for managing events from multiple sources. Key Concepts: EventBridge = serverless event bus for event-driven architectures Real-time triggers = invoke GenAI workflows when specific events occur Event-driven vs scheduled = EventBridge responds to events, not time intervals

questions.question: A large corporation wants to build an internal knowledge search system that allows employees to ask natural language questions about company policies, HR documents, and project documentation stored across SharePoint, Confluence, and Amazon S3. The solution should provide conversational answers with citations, not just document links. The company prefers a fully managed service with minimal setup.Which AWS service should the company use?
questions.explain: CORRECT: Amazon Q Business Amazon Q Business is a fully managed, generative AI-powered assistant specifically designed for enterprise use cases. It connects directly to common enterprise data sources (SharePoint, Confluence, S3, Salesforce, etc.) and provides conversational answers to employee questions with source citations. It goes beyond search by using foundation models to synthesize information and generate natural language responses, making it ideal for this scenario. INCORRECT: Amazon Kendra Amazon Kendra is an intelligent enterprise search service powered by machine learning. While it can index documents and provide relevant search results with excerpts, it returns ranked document links rather than synthesized conversational answers. Kendra excels at precision search but does not use generative AI to create natural language responses like Amazon Q Business does. INCORRECT: Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases is designed for developers to implement Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) in custom applications. While powerful, it requires developers to build and maintain the application layer, handle authentication, and create the user interface. Amazon Q Business provides all of this out of the box as a ready-to-deploy assistant. INCORRECT: Amazon OpenSearch Service Amazon OpenSearch Service is a search and analytics engine that can perform full-text search and vector search. However, it is an infrastructure service that requires significant setup, configuration, and custom application development to create a user-facing knowledge assistant. It does not provide the managed, conversational interface that Amazon Q Business offers. Key Concepts: Amazon Q Business = conversational AI assistant for enterprise knowledge Amazon Kendra = intelligent search with document links, not generative answers Q Business vs Kendra = generative synthesis vs precision search results

questions.question: A development team is building a serverless application where AWS Lambda functions need to invoke Amazon Bedrock foundation models to generate product descriptions. The security team requires that the Lambda functions follow the principle of least privilege when accessing AWS services.Which approach should the developer implement to grant the Lambda function access to Amazon Bedrock?
options: Attach an IAM role to the Lambda function with the bedrock:InvokeModel permission for the specific models being used
Embed AWS access keys and secret keys as environment variables in the Lambda function configuration
Grant the Lambda function's IAM role full Administrator access to ensure it can call all AWS services
Store IAM credentials in the Lambda function code and rotate them manually every 90 days
questions.explain: CORRECT: Attach an IAM role to the Lambda function with the bedrock:InvokeModel permission for the specific models being used This follows AWS security best practices and the principle of least privilege. Lambda execution roles automatically provide temporary credentials to the function at runtime. By granting only the bedrock:InvokeModel permission (and optionally scoping it to specific model ARNs), you ensure the Lambda function has exactly the permissions it needs and nothing more. This approach is secure, scalable, and requires no credential management. INCORRECT: Embed AWS access keys and secret keys as environment variables in the Lambda function configuration Hardcoding or storing long-term credentials (access keys) anywhere in your application is a critical security anti-pattern. These credentials can be exposed through logs, code repositories, or compromised environments. Lambda execution roles provide temporary credentials automatically, eliminating the need for static access keys entirely. INCORRECT: Grant the Lambda function's IAM role full Administrator access to ensure it can call all AWS services Administrator access ( : ) violates the principle of least privilege. If the Lambda function or its code is compromised, an attacker would have full access to your AWS account. Always grant only the minimum permissions necessary for the function to perform its intended task - in this case, just bedrock:InvokeModel. INCORRECT: Store IAM credentials in the Lambda function code and rotate them manually every 90 days This approach has multiple security flaws: credentials in code can be accidentally committed to version control, manual rotation is error-prone and operationally burdensome, and static credentials are inherently less secure than temporary credentials. Lambda execution roles eliminate the need for any credential management. Key Concepts: Least privilege = grant only the minimum permissions required Lambda execution roles = automatic temporary credentials, no key management bedrock:InvokeModel = specific IAM permission for calling Bedrock models

refs.label: Identity and access management for Amazon Bedrock
Defining Lambda function permissions with an execution role

questions.question: A healthcare company is building a GenAI application that processes sensitive patient data using Amazon Bedrock. Due to strict compliance requirements, the security team mandates that all traffic between the application (running on Amazon EC2 instances in a private subnet) and Amazon Bedrock must remain within the AWS network and never traverse the public internet.Which AWS feature should the developer implement to meet this requirement?
options: Configure VPC endpoints for Amazon Bedrock to enable private connectivity
Use AWS Direct Connect to establish a dedicated network connection to Bedrock
Configure an internet gateway in the VPC and use HTTPS encryption for Bedrock API calls
Deploy a NAT gateway to route Bedrock traffic through a private IP address
questions.explain: CORRECT: Configure VPC endpoints for Amazon Bedrock to enable private connectivity VPC endpoints allow you to privately connect your VPC to Amazon Bedrock without requiring an internet gateway, NAT device, VPN connection, or AWS Direct Connect. Traffic between your VPC and Bedrock stays within the AWS network and never traverses the public internet. This is the recommended approach for meeting compliance requirements that mandate private connectivity to AWS services. INCORRECT: Use AWS Direct Connect to establish a dedicated network connection to Bedrock AWS Direct Connect creates a dedicated network connection from your on-premises data center to AWS. However, it is not necessary for connecting resources already running in AWS (like EC2 instances) to AWS services like Bedrock. VPC endpoints provide a simpler, more cost-effective solution for private connectivity within AWS. Direct Connect would only be relevant if you needed to connect on-premises systems to Bedrock. INCORRECT: Configure an internet gateway in the VPC and use HTTPS encryption for Bedrock API calls While HTTPS encrypts data in transit, using an internet gateway means traffic flows over the public internet, which violates the security requirement. Even though the data is encrypted, the compliance mandate specifically requires that traffic must not traverse the public internet at all. VPC endpoints ensure traffic remains entirely within the AWS network. INCORRECT: Deploy a NAT gateway to route Bedrock traffic through a private IP address A NAT gateway allows instances in private subnets to access the internet for outbound connections. However, traffic through a NAT gateway still goes over the public internet to reach AWS services by default. To keep traffic private, you need VPC endpoints, not a NAT gateway. NAT gateways are useful for general internet access but do not provide the private connectivity required here. Key Concepts: VPC endpoints = private connectivity to AWS services, no internet gateway needed Traffic stays within AWS network = meets compliance for sensitive data NAT gateway vs VPC endpoint = public internet routing vs private AWS network

questions.question: A financial services company is deploying a GenAI application that uses Amazon Bedrock to generate investment recommendations. For regulatory compliance, the company must maintain a complete audit trail of all foundation model API calls, including who made the request, when it was made, and which model was invoked. This audit log must be stored securely and be available for compliance reviews.Which AWS service should the developer enable to meet this audit logging requirement?
questions.explain: CORRECT: AWS CloudTrail AWS CloudTrail records AWS API calls made in your account, including calls to Amazon Bedrock. CloudTrail logs capture critical information such as the IAM principal (who), timestamp (when), API action (what), and request parameters (which model, input prompts if enabled). These logs can be stored in Amazon S3 for long-term retention and are designed specifically for compliance auditing and security analysis. CloudTrail is the standard AWS service for maintaining audit trails of service usage. INCORRECT: Amazon CloudWatch Logs Amazon CloudWatch Logs is used for collecting and storing application logs, metrics, and operational data. While you can configure Amazon Bedrock to send model invocation logs to CloudWatch Logs (including request/response payloads), CloudTrail is specifically designed for audit trails of API activity. CloudWatch Logs focuses on application-level logging and monitoring, whereas CloudTrail provides the governance and compliance layer for tracking who accessed which AWS services. INCORRECT: AWS Config AWS Config tracks configuration changes to AWS resources over time (e.g., Ђ

). It is designed to monitor resource compliance and configuration drift, not to audit API calls or log foundation model invocations. CloudTrail is the appropriate service for tracking API activity and user actions. INCORRECT: Amazon S3 access logs S3 access logs record requests made to S3 buckets (e.g., object uploads, downloads). They do not capture API calls to Amazon Bedrock or other AWS services. While CloudTrail logs can be stored in S3 for retention, S3 access logs themselves are not designed for auditing service API usage across AWS. Key Concepts: CloudTrail = audit trail of AWS API calls, who/what/when logging Compliance requirements = CloudTrail provides governance and audit logs CloudWatch Logs vs CloudTrail = application logging vs API activity auditing

refs.label: Monitor Amazon Bedrock API calls using CloudTrail

questions.question: A startup is building two GenAI features for their application: a customer support chatbot that generates conversational responses, and a semantic search function that finds similar product descriptions based on meaning. The team wants to use Amazon Titan models on Amazon Bedrock for both features.Which Amazon Titan models should the team use for each feature?
options: Use Amazon Titan Text for the chatbot and Amazon Titan Embeddings for the semantic search
Use Amazon Titan Embeddings for both features since it can handle text generation and search
Use Amazon Titan Text for both features since it can generate responses and perform searches
Use Amazon Titan Embeddings for the chatbot and Amazon Titan Text for the semantic search
questions.explain: CORRECT: Use Amazon Titan Text for the chatbot and Amazon Titan Embeddings for the semantic search Amazon Titan Text models are designed for text generation tasks like creating conversational responses, summaries, and content. Amazon Titan Embeddings models convert text into numerical vector representations (embeddings) that capture semantic meaning, which is essential for similarity search and retrieval tasks. This combination correctly matches each model type to its intended use case. INCORRECT: Use Amazon Titan Embeddings for both features since it can handle text generation and search Amazon Titan Embeddings models only convert text into vector representations; they do not generate new text. They output numerical arrays (embeddings), not human-readable text. You cannot use an embeddings model to generate conversational chatbot responses. Embeddings are exclusively for vectorization tasks needed in semantic search and RAG workflows. INCORRECT: Use Amazon Titan Text for both features since it can generate responses and perform searches While Amazon Titan Text can generate text, it is not designed to create embeddings for semantic search. Text generation models output tokens (words), not the numerical vectors required for similarity comparisons. Semantic search requires specialized embedding models to convert text into vectors that can be compared using mathematical operations like cosine similarity. INCORRECT: Use Amazon Titan Embeddings for the chatbot and Amazon Titan Text for the semantic search This reverses the correct usage. Embeddings models cannot generate conversational text (they only output numbers), and text generation models cannot create the vector embeddings needed for semantic search. Each model type serves a specific, non-interchangeable purpose. Key Concepts: Titan Text = text generation (chatbots, summaries, content creation) Titan Embeddings = convert text to vectors for semantic search Embeddings vs generation = numerical vectors vs human-readable text

questions.question: A customer service chatbot built with Amazon Bedrock needs to maintain conversation history for each user session to provide contextually relevant responses. The application requires fast read and write access to recent messages, automatic scaling based on traffic, and the ability to query conversations by user ID. The development team wants a serverless database solution.Which AWS database service is best suited for storing and retrieving conversation history?
questions.explain: CORRECT: Amazon DynamoDB Amazon DynamoDB is a fully managed NoSQL database that provides single-digit millisecond latency at any scale. It is the ideal choice for storing conversation history because it automatically scales to handle varying loads, requires no server management, and supports fast key-value lookups (e.g., by session_id or user_id). The schema flexibility of DynamoDB makes it perfect for storing varying message structures in chat applications. INCORRECT: Amazon RDS PostgreSQL Amazon RDS is a managed relational database that requires capacity planning, instance sizing, and more operational overhead compared to DynamoDB. While RDS can store conversation data, it is over-engineered for simple key-value lookups and does not scale as seamlessly as DynamoDB for unpredictable chatbot traffic patterns. Relational databases are better suited for complex queries with joins, which are not needed for basic conversation history retrieval. INCORRECT: Amazon S3 Amazon S3 is object storage optimized for storing large files (images, videos, documents) with high durability. While you can store conversation logs in S3, it is not designed for low-latency, transactional read/write operations required during active chat sessions. S3 is better suited for archival storage or batch processing of historical conversations, not real-time session management. INCORRECT: Amazon ElastiCache Amazon ElastiCache (Redis or Memcached) is an in-memory caching service designed for sub-millisecond latency. While it can temporarily store conversation data for extremely fast access, it is volatile (data can be lost on restart) and expensive for long-term storage. ElastiCache is typically used as a caching layer in front of a persistent database like DynamoDB, not as the primary data store. Key Concepts: DynamoDB = serverless NoSQL, fast key-value lookups, automatic scaling Conversation history = session_id/user_id as partition key, messages as items S3 vs DynamoDB = archival storage vs transactional database

questions.question: A developer is building a customer support application using the Amazon Bedrock Converse API. The application needs to provide the foundation model with context about its role (act as a helpful support agent), include the user's question, and reference a previous response from the model. The developer wants to structure the conversation properly according to standard prompt formatting.Which message role structure should the developer use?
options: System message for instructions, User message for the question, Assistant message for the model's previous response
User message for instructions, System message for the question, Assistant message for context
Assistant message for instructions, System message for the question, User message for previous responses
All messages should use the User role since the model can infer context automatically
questions.explain: CORRECT: System message for instructions, User message for the question, Assistant message for the model's previous response This follows the standard conversation structure used by most foundation models. The System message sets the model's behavior and role (e.g., Ђ

). The User message contains the human's input or question. The Assistant message represents the model's previous output, which provides context for multi-turn conversations. This structure ensures the model understands its role, the user's intent, and the conversation history. INCORRECT: User message for instructions, System message for the question, Assistant message for context This reverses the proper roles. System messages are specifically designed for providing instructions and setting behavior, not for user questions. Putting instructions in a User message reduces their effectiveness because the model treats them as part of the user's input rather than foundational directives that guide all responses. INCORRECT: Assistant message for instructions, System message for the question, User message for previous responses This completely misuses all three roles. Assistant messages should only contain the model's own previous outputs, not instructions. Using Assistant for instructions would make the model think it already said those things, causing confusion. System messages are for instructions, not questions. INCORRECT: All messages should use the User role since the model can infer context automatically While some models might attempt to infer context, using proper message roles (System, User, Assistant) provides clear, unambiguous structure that improves model performance. Proper role distinction helps the model understand what is an instruction vs. a question vs. its own previous output, leading to more accurate and contextually appropriate responses. Key Concepts: System message = model instructions and behavior guidelines User message = human input, questions, requests Assistant message = model's previous responses (for conversation context)

refs.label: Carry out a conversation with the Converse API operations

questions.question: An e-commerce company wants to build a product recommendation system that analyzes both product images and text descriptions to suggest similar items to customers. The system needs a foundation model that can process and understand both visual and textual information simultaneously.Which type of Amazon Bedrock model should the developer use?
options: Text-only foundation model with separate computer vision service
Embedding model for both images and text
Image generation model like Amazon Titan Image Generator
questions.explain: CORRECT: Multimodal foundation model Multimodal foundation models (like Anthropic Claude 4,5 models on Amazon Bedrock) can process multiple types of input simultaneously, including images and text. These models are specifically designed to understand and reason about visual content alongside textual information, making them ideal for analyzing product images and descriptions together to generate recommendations. The model can Ђ

the image and read the text in a single API call. INCORRECT: Text-only foundation model with separate computer vision service While you could use a text-only model combined with Amazon Rekognition or Amazon Textract, this approach requires multiple service calls, additional integration complexity, and separate processing pipelines. The text model would receive labels or descriptions from the vision service but wouldn't truly Ђ

the image. Multimodal models provide a more elegant, integrated solution. INCORRECT: Embedding model for both images and text Embedding models (like Amazon Titan Embeddings) convert inputs into vector representations for similarity search. While some embedding models support both text and images, they output numerical vectors, not natural language recommendations or reasoning. You would still need a separate text generation model to create the actual product recommendations based on those embeddings. INCORRECT: Image generation model like Amazon Titan Image Generator Amazon Titan Image Generator creates new images from text descriptions (text-to-image). It does not analyze existing images or process visual information for understanding and reasoning. Image generation models work in the opposite direction from what's needed - they produce images rather than understanding them. Key Concepts: Multimodal models = process text + images in single API call Use cases = visual Q&A, image analysis with text context, content moderation Image generation vs image understanding = creating images vs analyzing images

questions.question: A legal firm is implementing a Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) system using Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases to answer questions about contracts stored in Amazon S3. The contracts are long PDF documents (50-100 pages each) with hierarchical structure including sections, subsections, and clauses. The team needs to split these documents into smaller pieces for effective retrieval.What is this process of splitting documents into smaller pieces called, and why is it necessary?
options: Chunking; it breaks documents into manageable segments that fit within the foundation model's context window and improves retrieval precision
Tokenization; it converts words into numbers that the model can process mathematically
Embedding; it transforms text into vector representations for semantic search
Indexing; it creates a searchable catalog of all documents in the knowledge base
questions.explain: CORRECT: Chunking; it breaks documents into manageable segments that fit within the foundation model's context window and improves retrieval precision Chunking is the process of dividing large documents into smaller, semantically meaningful segments (chunks). This is essential for RAG because: (1) foundation models have limited context windows and cannot process entire 100-page documents at once, (2) retrieving smaller, relevant chunks is more precise than retrieving entire documents, and (3) each chunk can be embedded and searched independently. Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases provides automatic chunking capabilities with configurable chunk sizes. INCORRECT: Tokenization; it converts words into numbers that the model can process mathematically Tokenization is a different process where text is broken into tokens (words or subwords) and converted into numerical IDs that the model's neural network can process. While tokenization happens internally when the model processes text, it is not the same as chunking documents for retrieval. Tokenization operates at the word level, while chunking operates at the paragraph or section level. INCORRECT: Embedding; it transforms text into vector representations for semantic search Embedding is the process of converting text chunks into numerical vector representations (arrays of numbers) that capture semantic meaning. While embeddings are created after chunking, they are not the chunking process itself. The workflow is: document ЃЁ chunking ЃЁ embedding ЃЁ storage in vector database. Embeddings enable semantic search but require the document to be chunked first. INCORRECT: Indexing; it creates a searchable catalog of all documents in the knowledge base Indexing is the process of organizing and storing chunks (and their embeddings) in a searchable structure (like a vector database index). Indexing happens after chunking and embedding. While indexing makes the chunks searchable, it is not the process of splitting the documents into those chunks in the first place. Key Concepts: Chunking = splitting documents into smaller segments for RAG Context window limitation = models can only process limited tokens at once Chunking ЃЁ Embedding ЃЁ Indexing = sequential RAG pipeline steps

questions.question: A financial services company is building a RAG system using Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases to answer employee questions about company policies. The policies are stored as PDF documents in Amazon S3, and some policies are department-specific (HR, Finance, Legal) while others have different effective dates. The team wants to improve search precision by allowing the system to filter results based on department and date when retrieving relevant policy excerpts.What should the developer add to improve the filtering capabilities of the vector search?
options: Metadata tags on the source documents in S3 (e.g., department, effective_date) that can be used as filters during retrieval
Longer chunk sizes to include more context in each retrieved segment
Multiple embedding models to create different vector representations
Higher dimensional embeddings to capture more semantic information
questions.explain: CORRECT: Metadata tags on the source documents in S3 (e.g., department, effective_date) that can be used as filters during retrieval Metadata provides additional structured information about documents or chunks that can be used to filter search results. By adding metadata like Ђ
or Ђ
to S3 objects or within Knowledge Base configurations, the system can combine semantic search (vector similarity) with metadata filtering. This enables queries like Ђ

to return only relevant, filtered results rather than all semantically similar content. INCORRECT: Longer chunk sizes to include more context in each retrieved segment Increasing chunk size might provide more context per chunk but does not help with filtering by department or date. Larger chunks can actually reduce retrieval precision because they contain more mixed information. Chunk size affects the granularity of retrieval, not the ability to filter by structured attributes like department or date. INCORRECT: Multiple embedding models to create different vector representations Using multiple embedding models creates different vector spaces but does not enable filtering by structured attributes like department or effective date. Multiple embeddings might capture different semantic aspects of the text, but they don't provide the structured filtering capabilities that metadata tags offer. This approach also increases complexity and cost. INCORRECT: Higher dimensional embeddings to capture more semantic information Higher dimensional embeddings (e.g., 1536 dimensions vs 768 dimensions) might capture more nuanced semantic relationships in the text, but they do not enable filtering by structured metadata fields. Dimensionality affects semantic similarity quality, not the ability to apply categorical or date-based filters. Metadata is the correct mechanism for structured filtering. Key Concepts: Metadata = structured attributes for filtering (department, date, author, etc.) Hybrid search = semantic similarity + metadata filtering S3 object metadata or custom attributes = source for filter criteria

questions.question: A news aggregation service wants to enhance their GenAI article summarization system by identifying and tagging key entities (people, organizations, locations, dates) mentioned in articles before sending them to Amazon Bedrock for summarization. This entity information will be used to create more structured summaries and enable filtering by entity type.Which AWS service should the developer use to automatically extract these entities from the text?
questions.explain: CORRECT: Amazon Comprehend Amazon Comprehend is a natural language processing (NLP) service that uses machine learning to extract insights from text, including named entity recognition (NER). It can automatically identify and categorize entities such as people, organizations, locations, dates, quantities, and more. Comprehend is the purpose-built service for analyzing text content and extracting structured information like entities before passing data to foundation models. INCORRECT: Amazon Textract Amazon Textract is a machine learning service that automatically extracts text, handwriting, and data from scanned documents and images (OCR - Optical Character Recognition). While Textract extracts text from visual documents, it does not perform semantic analysis or entity recognition on that text. You would use Textract to digitize scanned articles, then use Comprehend to extract entities from the resulting text. INCORRECT: Amazon Rekognition Amazon Rekognition is a computer vision service that analyzes images and videos to detect objects, faces, scenes, activities, and inappropriate content. It processes visual data, not text. Rekognition cannot read or analyze textual content in articles to extract named entities. It would only be relevant if you needed to analyze images accompanying the articles. INCORRECT: Amazon Transcribe Amazon Transcribe is an automatic speech recognition (ASR) service that converts audio and video into text. It is used for transcribing podcasts, meetings, customer calls, etc. While Transcribe can identify speakers and generate timestamps, it does not perform entity extraction or semantic analysis on the transcribed text. You would use Comprehend after Transcribe to extract entities from transcripts. Key Concepts: Amazon Comprehend = NLP service for entity extraction, sentiment, key phrases Named Entity Recognition (NER) = identifying people, organizations, locations, dates Textract vs Comprehend = OCR (image-to-text) vs NLP (text analysis)

questions.question: A media company is building a content recommendation engine that uses Amazon Bedrock embeddings to find similar articles based on semantic meaning. They need a fully managed vector database that can efficiently perform k-nearest neighbor (k-NN) searches across millions of article embeddings. The team wants a service that integrates natively with their existing search infrastructure and supports both keyword and vector search.Which AWS service should the team use as their vector database?
questions.explain: CORRECT: Amazon OpenSearch Service Amazon OpenSearch Service (with its k-NN plugin or Serverless Vector Engine) is a fully managed service that supports efficient vector storage and k-nearest neighbor searches at scale. It is specifically designed for semantic search use cases and can handle millions of embeddings with low-latency retrieval. OpenSearch also supports hybrid search, combining traditional keyword search with vector similarity search, making it ideal for content recommendation engines. INCORRECT: Amazon RDS PostgreSQL While Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL supports the pgvector extension for vector search, it is a relational database primarily designed for structured, transactional data with complex queries and joins. For a dedicated vector search workload at scale (millions of embeddings), OpenSearch provides better performance, lower latency, and more specialized vector search capabilities. PostgreSQL with pgvector is better suited when you need to combine vector search with relational data. INCORRECT: Amazon DynamoDB Amazon DynamoDB is a NoSQL key-value database optimized for fast transactional lookups by primary key. While you can store embeddings as attributes in DynamoDB, it does not natively support vector similarity search or k-NN algorithms. You would need to implement custom logic or use a separate vector index, which defeats the purpose. DynamoDB is excellent for storing metadata but not for performing vector similarity searches. INCORRECT: Amazon ElastiCache Amazon ElastiCache (Redis or Memcached) is an in-memory caching service designed for sub-millisecond access to frequently accessed data. While Redis supports vector search through extensions, ElastiCache is optimized for caching rather than persistent, large-scale vector storage. It is also more expensive than OpenSearch for storing millions of embeddings long-term. ElastiCache is better used as a caching layer, not the primary vector database. Key Concepts: OpenSearch Service = purpose-built for vector search at scale with k-NN k-NN search = finding nearest neighbors in vector space (semantic similarity) Hybrid search = combining keyword search + vector similarity search
